# Generator Dealer & Service Centre — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-generator-dealer-generator-dealer-service
**Vertical slug:** generator-dealer
**VN-ID:** VN-COM-ELE-001
**Niche family:** NF-COM-ELE standalone
**Family role:** standalone
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Generator sales and service is one of Nigeria's most essential industries — driven by NEPA/DISCO power supply failure averaging 10–20 hours of outage per day. Dealers sell Honda (EM/EB series), Mikano, Perkins, Elemax, Tiger, Sumec Firman, and Lutian generators. Capacities range from 1kVA portable units to 100kVA industrial. The inverter/solar hybrid market is growing but generators remain dominant for backup. Service centres offer repairs, oil changes, AVR replacement, carburetor cleaning, and fuel pump servicing. Church, estate, hospital, and hotel markets are the biggest bulk buyers. Generator importation through Apapa Port and Cotonou supply chains is the primary supply route. NEPZA and SON conformity requirements apply to imports.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Pastor Biodun Adeleke, 52, Church Admin, Ibadan** — Managing power for 3 services daily. Needs 50kVA+ Mikano/Perkins, service contract, diesel supply link.
- **Olumide Bello, 38, Estate Facility Manager, Lekki** — Estate of 120 flats needs backup power. Needs two 60kVA sets, maintenance SLA, 24/7 repair hotline.
- **Chidinma Okoye, 29, Shop Owner, Onitsha** — Small shop needs a 3kVA Honda for AC and POS machine. Needs affordable unit, payment plan, warranty.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home:** Hero (power Nigeria never stops), generator categories, service CTA, WhatsApp
- **Products:** By capacity (1–5kVA, 6–20kVA, 21–60kVA, 60kVA+), brand showcase
- **Services:** Repairs, maintenance, spare parts, emergency call-out
- **Brands:** Honda, Mikano, Perkins, Elemax, Sumec Firman, Tiger — with NGN price ranges
- **About:** CAC, SON, years in business, workshop location
- **Contact:** WhatsApp, emergency service line, address, NDPR notice

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Reliable, urgent, problem-solving. "When NEPA fails, we don't." Primary CTA: "Get a Quote on WhatsApp". Secondary CTA: "Book a Service Call". Reference NEPA/DISCO outage reality directly — this resonates deeply with Nigerian customers.

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- SON Conformity Assessment for imported generators
- CAC business registration
- Authorised dealership badges (Honda, Mikano, Perkins)
- Emergency service hotline (24/7)
- Warranty terms (12–24 months)
- Customer testimonials (churches, estates)
- Payment: Paystack, bank transfer, POS, instalment/hire purchase

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Large Mikano generator set in church generator room — industrial setting, Lagos
2. Technician servicing Honda generator — workshop, branded uniform
3. Showroom floor with generators of various sizes — price tags visible
4. Generator running during power outage — backup power for a Lagos business
5. Delivery of 60kVA set to estate — crane/flatbed truck, security post in background
6. Spare parts shelf — carburetors, AVRs, fuel filters neatly arranged
7. Customer signing service contract — professional interaction, reception desk

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- SON Conformity Assessment Certificate
- CAC business name/limited company registration
- DPR fuel handling permit (if diesel supply service included)
- EPA/LASEPA emissions compliance notice
- NDPR consent in service booking forms

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp CTA on home page and contact page (emergency text format)
- [x] All prices in NGN (₦) — per kVA range
- [x] Nigerian cities (Ibadan, Lekki, Onitsha) as customer examples
- [x] Nigerian names in testimonials (Pastor Biodun, Olumide, Chidinma)
- [x] SON + brand authorisation trust signals
- [x] Payment: Paystack, bank transfer, POS, hire purchase option
- [x] Mobile-first 375px layout
- [x] esc() on all user-supplied strings
- [x] NDPR notice in service booking forms
